item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This SCiP has been translated into Chinese.
+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Really enjoyed this skip up until the second interview.
The second interview is completely unnecessary and feels more like something that belongs in The Leak.

Novote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCP-3609: Yes. I was so near the accomplishment of my purpose. I successfully threw Máni off his chariot during my previous round of pursuit, and was about to devour him. But ten beams of light from the East consumed us, and then I find myself standing under the sky of darkness and on that lifeless land. Máni was nowhere in sight.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A great article but I don't know how this crosslinks with SCP-3609.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My issues:

+ show block
– hide block
  • A composite of distorted beliefs regarding an ancient deity probably shouldn't speak in English. I feel like we ought to have note of this being a translation (maybe even have communication with it difficult because of this; it speaks a composite of several ancient languages all meshed together, requiring numerous linguistic experts to untangle what it's saying.)
  • There's a lot of ellipsis used here. They started to grate on me pretty quickly.
  • The last interview felt very exposition-heavy.

That being said: As before, I really, really love the fundamental premise here. I found the final interview too forced, but the actual information in the interview — the revelation of someone actually trying to "recreate" their God from a pastiche of distortions and misconceptions presented over time — is one I really like. The reference to a 'broken god' is cute, too (I'm glad you didn't cross-link that with anything; I think that would have taken it from 'cute' to 'eye-roll').

That, combined with the polish of the description, got me to (tentatively) upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think the balance between story and exposition here leans too far into the latter. I would like to see more imagery and history and less of the containment procedures and such seeming to bleed into the main article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel like the interview log leaned a bit too much on infodump, but I do enjoy the idea of a god that's been ruined by the different views people have on them. The interview with the god was also solidly done.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This draft has been languishing for far too long in my sandbox(s). Special thanks to Grimmcreeper, DrMagnus, Dmatix, Ninevolt, TheGreatHippo, Taylor Itkin, Tufto, and Zyn for looking over the draft.

Image Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Baal_(demon)#/media/File:Bael.jpg, public domain.
